context.summary: Use count-controlled loops and a sequence to help create a dance routine project on your BBC micro:bit. You can use the project alone or with friends.
how_it_works: The program starts when you press button A.
Arrows appear on the micro:bitâs LED display, which tell you how to dance â step left then right, put your hands up in the air, then point down to the floor.
If you are using the project in a group, everyone should press button A on their micro:bits at the same time, so that you start the dance routine at the same time and remain in sync.
After showing each image, the programÂ pausesÂ for a second (1000 milliseconds) before showing the next image. The pause helps everyone to keep in time.
The sequence of dance moves repeats four times then stops. The sequence is kept going using a count-controlledÂ loop. Count-controlled loops repeat instructions a set number of times.
Using loops to keep things happening is an important idea in computer programming. This is also called iteration.Â
When the routine is finished, the micro:bitâs LED display is cleared.
improve_it: Make instructions for other dance sequences to appear when you use other inputs such as pressing button B or shaking the micro:bit.
Change the length of the pauses to make the program run in time with your favourite song.
Use this project alongside the step counter project to monitor how well you dance.

micropython.source:
# Imports go at the top
from microbit import *


while True:
    if button_a.is_pressed():
        for i in range(4):  
            display.show(Image('00900:'
                       '09000:'
                       '99999:'
                       '09000:'
                       '00900'))
            sleep(1000)
            display.show(Image('00900:'
                       '00090:'
                       '99999:'
                       '00090:'
                       '00900'))
            sleep(1000)
            display.show(Image('00900:'
                       '09990:'
                       '90909:'
                       '00900:'
                       '00900'))
            sleep(1000)
            display.show(Image('00900:'
                       '00900:'
                       '90909:'
                       '09990:'
                       '00900'))
            sleep(1000)
    display.clear()
